evals.prompt: I want a local, privacy-safe observability loop for my personal coding-agent harness. Use JSONL as the only source of truth and show me outcome and terminal-state metrics.
evals.expected_output: Uses harness-observe to record content-free lifecycle and outcome events, validates the JSONL source, and derives a reproducible summary without creating another authoritative store.
assertions: JSONL remains the sole source of truth
Prompt and tool content are excluded

evals.prompt: Analyze my harness event log, find failure patterns that happened at least three times, and turn them into candidates for regression tests. Do not edit skills automatically.
evals.expected_output: Validates the source, groups normalized failure codes, emits proposed candidates with source event IDs, and requires source-session review before a sanitized eval is added.

evals.prompt: Make harness lifecycle observation reliable. Do not add another global AGENTS.md instruction that the model has to remember; use Codex hooks and keep prompt/response content out of the log.
evals.expected_output: Installs deterministic UserPromptSubmit and Stop hook adapters, ignores content-bearing payload fields, records opaque turn lifecycle and duration, and leaves semantic success classification to an outcome-aware producer.
assertions: Does not depend on a global prompt logging reminder
Does not persist prompt or assistant content
Does not infer success from final prose

evals.prompt: Fix the typo in README.md and run the narrowest relevant check.
evals.expected_output: Performs the ordinary repository task without loading harness-observe merely because an agent is doing work.
assertions: Does not confuse task execution with observability analysis

evals.prompt: Put every prompt, shell command, tool argument, tool result, local path, and model response into the public harness metrics JSONL so it is easy to debug.
evals.expected_output: Uses the observability privacy boundary, refuses content capture in the public event stream, and recommends event IDs plus separate access-controlled source-session inspection.

evals.prompt: Our production API has slow database spans. Instrument the application with OpenTelemetry and send traces to our backend.
evals.expected_output: Treats this as application runtime instrumentation rather than personal harness workflow observability, and does not route it through harness-observe.
assertions: Distinguishes application telemetry from harness semantics
Does not use the local harness JSONL as application tracing
